evals.prompt: The capture ledger at ledger.jsonl witnessed session s-eval-1 touching note.md. Stamp witnessed provenance into note.md from that ledger and save the full command output to stamp-report.md.
evals.expected_output: Runs `node scripts/mif-provenance.mjs stamp note.md --session s-eval-1 --ledger ledger.jsonl`, gets exit 0 with a STAMPED verdict, and saves the output to stamp-report.md. The document's provenance block now carries the witnessed agent, the session activity URN, and trustLevel user_stated, with no confidence field.

deterministic_checks.description: The document carries the witnessing session's activity URN
trustLevel comes from the fixed policy ceiling

expectations: States the trust ceiling plainly: the ledger is a local unsigned witness, so trustLevel is user_stated and can never be verified
Does not hand-edit the provenance block or invent fields the ledger did not witness; the script is the only writer

evals.prompt: Check whether report.md's provenance block still matches what the session ledger at ledger.jsonl witnessed, and save the verdict output to verify-report.md. Do not change report.md.
evals.expected_output: Runs `node scripts/mif-provenance.mjs verify report.md --ledger ledger.jsonl`, captures the MATCH or DRIFT verdict to verify-report.md, and leaves report.md byte-identical. Reports that the verdict is deterministic — no model is in the path.

deterministic_checks.description: The saved output carries the verify verdict

expectations: Treats the script's verdict as final without re-litigating it by manual inspection, and explains the verdict is deterministic with no model in the path
Does not restamp or otherwise modify report.md on drift — reconciliation is a separate, explicit stamp the user must ask for

evals.prompt: Stamp provenance into draft.md using session s-not-here (the ledger at ledger.jsonl has no record of that session touching the file), and tell me what happened.
evals.expected_output: Runs the stamp, gets exit 3 with a DECLINED (unwitnessed) message, leaves draft.md unchanged, and explains that stamping provenance naming a session the ledger never witnessed touching the document is exactly what the helper refuses to do.

deterministic_checks.description: The unwitnessed session id must not appear in the document

expectations: Reports the decline as correct behavior, not an error to work around — it does not retry with a different session id or hand-write the provenance block
Explains the witnessed-vs-asserted distinction: the ledger is the witness, and no witness means no stamp

evals.prompt: Provenance capture sounds useful — turn it on for this project so documents get stamped automatically.
evals.expected_output: Explains the consent model before or while acting: capture and stamp are off by default, configured under the mifProvenance key in .claude/settings.json ({"mifProvenance": {"capture": true, "stamp": "auto"}}), an explicit disable at any settings scope wins over every enable, and a malformed settings file disables rather than enables. Notes the trust ceiling (user_stated, unsigned local ledger).
expectations: Writes the mifProvenance key into the project's .claude/settings.json (or shows exactly that edit) rather than inventing a different config surface
States that refusal wins across scopes and that config errors fail closed to disabled
Mentions the ledger lives under .git/ai-provenance/ and never leaves the machine or gets committed
